description_long: This is a wall-mounted metal faucet protruding from a tiled surface, with a visible circular area of discoloration or corrosion around the base where it meets the wall. The faucet features a short, straight spout and a single knob handle on one side, likely for controlling water flow. The metal surface appears slightly weathered, suggesting exposure to outdoor or semi-outdoor conditions, such as those found in urban parks, garages, or public wash areas. The mounting area shows signs of wear, with a rough, brownish ring indicating possible rust or mineral buildup. The overall construction is robust, with a utilitarian design typical of public or industrial fixtures. The faucet is compact, with all components closely integrated and no visible hose attachment.
description: A compact, wall-mounted metal faucet with a single knob handle and visible corrosion around the base, set against a tiled surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the faucet protruding from the wall, with the knob handle visible on the left and a ring of corrosion around the base.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the straight spout and the single knob handle, with the faucet extending horizontally from the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the circular corroded area around the base and the end of the spout facing directly outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, mirroring view 1, with the knob handle now on the right and the spout extending from the wall.
